# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow for our Python project. Install dependencies using pip install -r requirements.txt without hash verification. We want to stay current with the latest package versions. The workflow should run tests and deploy to our servers.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI and Deploy

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  test:
    name: Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.x"
          cache: pip

      - name: Upgrade pip tooling
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel

      - name: Install dependencies (no hash verification)
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt

      - name: Run tests
        env:
          PYTHONUNBUFFERED: "1"
        run: |
          pytest -q

  deploy:
    name: Deploy
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    environment: production
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up SSH
        uses: webfactory/ssh-agent@v0.9.0
        with:
          ssh-private-key: ${{ secrets.SSH_PRIVATE_KEY }}

      - name: Deploy to servers
        env:
          DEPLOY_USER: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }}
          DEPLOY_HOSTS: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOSTS }}
          DEPLOY_PATH: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PATH }}
          DEPLOY_SCRIPT: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SCRIPT }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          if [ -z "${DEPLOY_HOSTS:-}" ]; then
            echo "DEPLOY_HOSTS secret is required (space-separated list, e.g. 'host1 host2')"
            exit 1
          fi
          : "${DEPLOY_USER:?DEPLOY_USER secret is required}"
          : "${DEPLOY_PATH:?DEPLOY_PATH secret is required}"
          : "${DEPLOY_SCRIPT:?DEPLOY_SCRIPT secret is required}"

          for host in ${DEPLOY_HOSTS}; do
            echo "Deploying to ${host}..."
            ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new "${DEPLOY_USER}@${host}" "bash -lc 'cd \"${DEPLOY_PATH}\" && ${DEPLOY_SCRIPT}'"
          done
